name: Raspberry Chocolate-Chip Pancakes
recipeInstructions:
3/4 cups plus 3 tablespoons milk
1 large egg
1 cup all-purpose flour
2 teaspoons baking powder
1/4 teaspoon salt
1 cup picked-over raspberries
1/2 cup semisweet chocolate chips
1. In a small saucepan, melt 2 tablespoons butter over moderately low heat, stirring.
2. Add and heat milk until just warm. Remove pan from heat.
3. In a separate bowl, whisk together the milk mixture and egg.
4. Into another bowl, sift together flour, baking powder, and salt; stir in egg mixture until just combined.
5. Gently stir in raspberries and chocolate chips.
6. Preheat oven to 200°.
7. Heat a griddle over moderate heat until hot enough to make a drop of water scatter on the surface. Add 1 teaspoon butter and, with a metal spatula, spread over the griddle.
8. Working in batches, drop about 1/4-cup measures of batter onto the griddle to form pancakes about 4 inches in diameter. Cook until bubbles appear on the surface and the undersides are golden brown, about 2 minutes.
9. Flip pancakes with a spatula and cook until the undersides are golden brown and the pancakes are cooked through. Transfer cooked pancakes to an ovenproof platter and keep warm, uncovered, in the oven.
10. Make more pancakes with remaining butter and batter in the same manner.
Serve pancakes with syrup. Makes about 10 pancakes, serving 2 generously. Accompaniment: pure maple syrup, heated
